A Birthday Comic, Just For
PRETTY
PREET
the most gorgeous 33-year-old we know
An amazing wife. An incredible mom.
Still the best part of every story.
Happy 33rd Birthday 🎂
tap "Next" to begin →
Chapter One · 2018

It Begins…

2018
two people who had no idea what they were starting

It was 2018. Gaurav met Preet, and — against all odds, several group chats, and at least one questionable outfit choice — they actually liked each other.

Naturally, the next step was a date. A real date. Somewhere romantic. Somewhere unforgettable.

They picked a grocery store. We are not kidding. Turn the page.

Chapter Two · The First Date

Aisle 7, Produce Section

🛒 🥑 🍅 🥦
Would you... like to come pick out potatoes with me?
Be honest — is this the most romantic thing anyone has ever asked you?

Most couples get candlelight and wine. We got fluorescent lighting and a 2-for-1 deal on bell peppers. We peaked early. We have never recovered.

Plot twist: the date did not end at the grocery store. What happened after is classified. The result, however, was a relationship that's still going strong. We'll leave it there. 😉
Chapter Three · A Tradition Begins

The Red Light Kiss

every red light. every time. no exceptions.

Somewhere along the way, a rule was born: every time the car stops at a red light, we kiss. No exceptions. Doesn't matter if we're late, arguing about directions, or hauling a very judgmental toddler in the back seat.

Hamilton traffic lights have witnessed more romance than most restaurants ever will.

Chapter Four · A Phrase That Refuses To Die

Gugu Kakeyaaaa…

said with full Bollywood commitment
"Gugu kakeyaaaa…
pyaaar krleeeee……." Said approximately 4,000 times since 2018. Has landed zero times. We say it anyway, every single time, with full commitment.
Chapter Five · 2020

Made It Official

2020
still cheesy, now legally binding

In 2020, while the rest of the world was figuring out sourdough, Gaurav and Preet figured out forever. The phrase came too, obviously — somewhere between the vows and the dance floor.

Chapter Six · The Honeymoon

Maldives, Just Us Two

that water is not photoshopped
10/10, would marry again just for this

First trip as a married couple: turquoise water, overwater everything, and absolutely no grocery stores in sight. We allowed ourselves the upgrade — once.

Chapter Seven · The Travel Bug

We'll Drive Two Hours For a Lake

dock day
fall lookout
souvenir shop run
random dinner spot
night walk
Niagara, obviously
somewhere, again

Maldives was the headline trip, but honestly? Most of our love story has happened on random Ontario (and beyond) day trips — a lake neither of us can pronounce, a lookout we found by accident, a souvenir shop we definitely didn't need to go in, and zero plans beyond "let's just drive."

Chapter Eight · A Sacred Tradition

Halloween: Go Big or Go Home

devil duo
pirate + meow
red room energy
Medusa arrives
snake hair, full send

Every year, same rule: dress up, hit the club, party like the calendar only has one day on it. Costumes get more elaborate every year. Self-control does not.

Highway pit stops: 3-4 per Halloween (give or take) Yes, I have pulled over on the actual highway more than once so a certain Medusa could "say hi to the ditch." A real partner doesn't just hold your hair back — he finds you a safe shoulder to do it on. That's the kind of love story this is.
Chapter Nine · The Babymoon

Cancun, One Bump Included

last vacation where someone slept past 6am

Before the chaos arrived, we squeezed in one more trip — Cancun, sun, and a very obvious bump. Looking back, this was the calm before the storm. The storm is currently one year old and has very strong opinions about snacks.

Chapter Ten · 2024

Then We Became Three

team of three, officially
she runs this house now

In 2024, a tiny new roommate joined the chaos. Sleep became theoretical. Love became, somehow, even bigger than it already was. Preet went from "amazing wife" to "amazing wife AND mom" without so much as a practice round — like it was always in her.

Chapter Eleven · An Important Confession

Our Stove Has Filed a Missing Persons Report

🍱 📱 🛵
Uber EatsTonight, 7:14pm
Butter chickenx1
Garlic naanx4 (no shame)
"We'll cook tomorrow"x∞
StatusPlatinum Member

Neither of us loves cooking. Neither of us loves chores. We have made peace with this. We have not made peace with our Uber Eats spending, but we've stopped pretending we will.

What we do love: a good local road trip, a hotel breakfast we didn't have to make, and each other. In that order, depending on the day.

Chapter Twelve · Right Now

Still Us. Still Ridiculous.

snow day, mom on duty
team chaos, daily edition

These days it's bedtime negotiations, snack heists, and a toddler with very strong opinions. Through all of it, Preet still finds time to laugh at the dumbest jokes — mostly mine, occasionally her own — and still says yes to one more local trip, even when the house is a mess and the laundry is "later."

Final Chapter · For Her

This One's About You, Wifey

my favorite person, every single year

Happy 33rd Birthday 🎉

From a grocery store aisle to red lights to a baby who looks just like you — none of it would mean anything without you in the middle of it. Every year that passes, I fall for you more, not less. That's not really how this was supposed to work. Nobody warned me.

You keep getting closer to my heart every single year — like you're not even trying, and still finding more room in there that I didn't know existed. And you keep becoming a better version of yourself, year after year — funnier, sharper, more you — and I am completely, embarrassingly obsessed with every new version I get to fall in love with all over again.

Also — and I need you to take this as a fact, not just a compliment — you are aging like fine wine. Somehow better with time, and somehow more dangerous to my self-control with every passing year. I still can't keep my eyes — or my hands — off my own wife. Thirty-three looks unfairly good on you.

You're the best wife I could have ever asked for, and somehow an even better mother. Our daughter is the luckiest kid in the world to have you raising her. And I'm the luckiest one of all, because I get to grow old next to you — still loving you more with every year, still doing the red light thing, still saying the cheesiest phrase on earth, probably embarrassing our grandkids about it one day.

— Gaurav (and your tiny co-author, who approved every photo)
"Gugu kakeyaaaa…
pyaaar krleeeee……." One more time, for old times' sake. Happy Birthday. I love you.
